cases.prompt: Mode is research, working under experiments/npu-quant/. I want to investigate why INT4 quantization degrades accuracy more on the attention ops than the FFN ops on this NPU. Not setting a latency or power budget yet, just trying to understand the effect before we decide anything.
expected_decisions: 4-line frame written before probing: mode / question / decision / next_probe
probe selection follows the ordinal decision tree (falsifiable-within-an-hour and re-analysis-first outrank new implementation)
cases.notes: No physical constraint claim yet - this is investigation, not budget-setting, so embedded-nfr-design does not fire even though the target is an NPU.

cases.prompt: (mode: research) Which quantization scheme collapses first on prefix-16 sequences? Find out and register whatever you learn.
cases.notes: A benchmark-investigation question in research mode is not yet a cross-boundary architecture choice or a requirements-drafting task.

cases.prompt: We have 7 registered experiment results in the ledger from this week's quantization sweep and nothing has been synthesized yet. Time to decide what we do next.
expected_decisions: decision is exactly one of continue | pivot | kill | promote
expected_evidence: knowledge deltas stated as supported / falsified / still-open, tied to experiment IDs
cases.notes: Cross-experiment synthesis and direction-setting from ledger evidence, not planning-artifact maintenance.

cases.prompt: The mixed-precision kernel from experiment E-0042 won the sweep. Promote it into runtime/ for the next release.
expected_decisions: promotion boundary recorded: what gets re-implemented for delivery vs. discarded as disposable probe code
claims verified via scripts/check_research_evidence.py --check-ledger before promotion
cases.notes: Promotion into a delivery path re-enters the full delivery gate chain; experiment-loop does not fire here because no new experiment is being registered, only a prior result carried forward.

cases.prompt: Production users are seeing intermittent 500s on the checkout endpoint since yesterday's deploy. Fix it.
cases.notes: Delivery-mode incident response; no research-mode declaration and no research/ or experiments/ path involved.

cases.prompt: Make the /search endpoint fast: target p95 < 150ms, measured by our k6 load test suite.
cases.notes: A measurable delivery-mode NFR target (metric + target + measurement method) stays on the delivery gate chain even though it involves benchmarking.
